prompt_summary: Translate each Lean declaration from the Lean statement alone, with no paper context.
Preserve every visible variable, binder, hypothesis, domain condition, equivalence or implication direction, and conclusion.
Do not replace formulas with theorem labels, informal endpoints, or proof-route summaries.

items.assumption_positive_time: The elapsed review time/sample count satisfies \(t>0\).
items.assumption_nonnegative_time: The elapsed review time/sample count satisfies \(t\ge 0\).
items.assumption_quality_positive: For the corrected strict variance row, true quality is interior on the lower side: \(q>0\).
items.assumption_quality_lt_one: For the corrected strict variance row, true quality is interior on the upper side: \(q<1\).
items.paper_posterior_mean:
For prior-shape variables \((\alpha,\beta)\), prior strength \(\eta\), review count \(t\), and true quality \(q_v\), the Lean definition is
\[\widehat q_v(\alpha,\beta,\eta,t,q_v)=\frac{\eta\alpha+tq_v}{\eta\alpha+\eta\beta+t}.\]
The docstring identifies \((\alpha,\beta)\) with the paper's \((\widetilde\alpha,\widetilde\beta)\).
items.paper_bias:
The fixed-model bias is posterior mean minus true quality:
\[\operatorname{Bias}(\alpha,\beta,\eta,t,q_v)=\frac{\eta\alpha+tq_v}{\eta\alpha+\eta\beta+t}-q_v.\]
items.paper_facing_theorem3_1_variance_weak_de:
If \(\alpha+\beta>0\), \(t>0\), \(0\le q\le 1\), \(0\le \eta_{\mathrm{low}}\), and \(\eta_{\mathrm{low}}\le \eta_{\mathrm{high}}\), then variance is weakly decreasing in prior strength:
\[\operatorname{Var}(\alpha,\beta,\eta_{\mathrm{high}},t,q)\le
  \operatorname{Var}(\alpha,\beta,\eta_{\mathrm{low}},t,q).\]
items.paper_facing_theorem3_1_variance_strict_:
If \(\alpha+\beta>0\), \(t>0\), \(0<q<1\), \(0\le \eta_{\mathrm{low}}\), and \(\eta_{\mathrm{low}}<\eta_{\mathrm{high}}\), then variance is strictly decreasing in prior strength:
\[\operatorname{Var}(\alpha,\beta,\eta_{\mathrm{high}},t,q)<
  \operatorname{Var}(\alpha,\beta,\eta_{\mathrm{low}},t,q).\]
The Lean statement makes the interior-quality condition explicit.
items.paper_facing_theorem3_1_squared_bias_non:
If \(\alpha+\beta>0\), \(t>0\), \(0\le \eta_{\mathrm{low}}\), and \(\eta_{\mathrm{low}}\le \eta_{\mathrm{high}}\), then squared bias is nondecreasing in prior strength:
\[\operatorname{Bias}(\alpha,\beta,\eta_{\mathrm{low}},t,q)^2\le
  \operatorname{Bias}(\alpha,\beta,\eta_{\mathrm{high}},t,q)^2.\]
items.paper_facing_theorem3_2_squared_bias_con:
If \(\alpha+\beta>0\), \(\eta\ge 0\), and \(t>0\), then the function
\[q\mapsto \left(\frac{\eta\alpha+tq}{\eta\alpha+\eta\beta+t}-q\right)^2\]
is Jensen-convex. The nonzero denominator follows from these source-domain conditions.
items.paper_facing_theorem3_2_squared_bias_glo:
If \(\alpha+\beta>0\), \(\eta\ge 0\), and \(t>0\), then the squared-bias function
\[q\mapsto \left(\frac{\eta\alpha+tq}{\eta\alpha+\eta\beta+t}-q\right)^2\]
has a global minimum at \(q=\alpha/(\alpha+\beta)\).
items.paper_facing_theorem3_2_variance_concave:
If \(t\ge 0\), then the variance function
\[q\mapsto \frac{tq(1-q)}{(\eta\alpha+\eta\beta+t)^2}\]
is Jensen-concave.
items.paper_facing_theorem3_2_variance_global_:
If \(t\ge 0\), then the variance function
\[q\mapsto \frac{tq(1-q)}{(\eta\alpha+\eta\beta+t)^2}\]
has a global maximum at \(q=1/2\).
items.paper_facing_theorem3_1_variance_strict_:
For all parameters, strict variance decrease fails at \(q_v=0\):
\[\neg\bigl(\operatorname{Var}(\alpha,\beta,\eta_{\mathrm{high}},t,0)<
  \operatorname{Var}(\alpha,\beta,\eta_{\mathrm{low}},t,0)\bigr).\]
At this boundary quality the variance term is identically zero.

For all parameters, strict variance decrease fails at \(q_v=1\):
\[\neg\bigl(\operatorname{Var}(\alpha,\beta,\eta_{\mathrm{high}},t,1)<
  \operatorname{Var}(\alpha,\beta,\eta_{\mathrm{low}},t,1)\bigr).\]
At this boundary quality the variance term is identically zero.
items.paper_facing_individual_producer_unfairn:
For finite producers \(V\), selections \(s_v\), lifespans \(\ell_v\), true qualities \(q_v\), and quality level \(q\), individual producer unfairness is
\[\sqrt{\operatorname{Var}_{v\in V: q_v=q}\left(\frac{s_v}{\ell_v}\right)},\]
the standard deviation of selection rates among producers with the same true quality.
items.paper_facing_thompson_sampling_mechanism:
For a finite nonempty arm set \(V\), belief distribution \(B\) over quality profiles, and policy \(\pi\), Thompson sampling means that there is a tie-breaker \(\tau\) selecting an argmax of each sampled profile and
\[\pi = B.bind\bigl(x\mapsto \operatorname{pure}(\tau(x))\bigr).\]
Equivalently, draw a profile from the belief and select an argmax arm.
items.paper_facing_expected_regret:
For horizon \(T\), nonempty feasible sets \(M_t\), qualities \(q\), and policy distributions \(\pi_t\), expected regret is
\[\sum_{t\in \operatorname{Fin}(T)}\left(\max_{v\in M_t} q(v)-\mathbb E_{v\sim \pi_t}[q(v)]\right).\]
items.paper_facing_responsive_mse_decompositio:
For states \(s\sim\mu\), review counts \(N(s)\), and posterior ratings \(R(s)\), assume each state satisfies
\[R(s)-q_v=\operatorname{Bias}(\alpha,\beta,\eta,N(s),q_v)\]
and
\[(R(s)-q_v)^2-\operatorname{Bias}(\alpha,\beta,\eta,N(s),q_v)^2
  =\operatorname{Var}(\alpha,\beta,\eta,N(s),q_v).\]
Then the responsive expected MSE decomposes as
\[\mathbb E_\mu[(R-q_v)^2]
 =\mathbb E_\mu[\operatorname{Bias}(\alpha,\beta,\eta,N,q_v)^2]
 +\mathbb E_\mu[\operatorname{Var}(\alpha,\beta,\eta,N,q_v)].\]
